definitions: A model used in chemistry to predict the geometry of individual molecules from the number of electron pairs surrounding their central atoms.
examples: Valence shell electron pair repulsion theory helps explain why water molecules have a bent shape.
Understanding valence shell electron pair repulsion theory is crucial for predicting molecular structures.
The principles of valence shell electron pair repulsion theory are fundamental in inorganic chemistry.
lemma: valence shell electron pair repulsion theory
source: valence shell electron pair repulsion theory
